commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh And they spoke unto him, saying, If you will be a servant unto this people this day, and will serve them, and answer them, and speak good words to them, then they will be your servants forever. Hebrew, "he said. "The transcribers, probably not understanding what they wrote, frequently make singular for plural verbs. 21, "They came "instead of he came. Some manuscripts and the ancient versions are correct. (Kennicott) Yield. Hebrew, "serve. "By the submission of one day he might have acquired the kingdom. Great attention is requisite at first. Tacitus (Hist. ) represents Vespasian, Novo principatu suspensum,vultus quoque ac sermones omnium circumspectantem.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
